Prostate Cancer Centre names former councillor Davison as new CEO   The Calgary Prostate Cancer Centre board has announced the appointment of Jeff Davison as its new CEO.

Canadians' fury over Rogers outage may complicate its merger hopes   TORONTO — Rogers Communications complicated its chances of getting antitrust approval for a C$20 billion telecom merger after Friday’s massive outage highlighted the perils of Canada’s effective telecom monopoly and sparked a backlash against its industry dominance.

Opinion: There is a better way than utility bill rebates in Alberta   Politicians in Alberta are lining up to announce rebates or caps for utility bills. The government has just doubled its electricity rebates, now six $50 rebates for a total of $600 million over the next six months, and is planning a cap on natural gas rates for next winter.
Braid: Poilievre gets the cheers, Charest faced with boos at big CPC event   Poilievre’s emotional fixation on grievances has far more appeal

Your letters for July 9, 2022   Taxes upon fees upon charges Has anyone really looked at their Enmax utility bill? I did. I paid $64 in actual costs for electricity, water, gas and garbage collection and $179 in hidden taxes called administration charges, delivery charges, distribution charges, transmission charges, variable charges, etc, etc. You can bet that there are profits built into each one of these additional charges.

Opinion: Kudos to Gondek, but 'Your Worship' isn't the only municipal term with a colonial past   Calgary Mayor Jyoti Gondek said on Tuesday that she would prefer no longer to be addressed as “Your Worship,” the traditional form of address for a mayor, but rather as “Mayor Gondek.”

Braid: Federal Conservative candidate Charest promises Alberta a new deal in Canada   Charest is the first federal Conservative leadership candidate to make specific Alberta promises that may jolt other Canadians
True Crime Byline: We should have seen JoAnn Wilson's murder coming   Leader-Post city editor Barb Pacholik recalls the many turns of the Colin Thatcher case across decades.

True Crime Byline: How one childhood incident pushed Dawn Crey into a downward spiral from which she would never escape   The new podcast True Crime Byline looks back at The Vancouver Sun investigation into Vancouver's missing women, a case that eventually led to the arrest and conviction of Robert Pickton. This profile of missing woman Dawn Crey was part of a series of investigative stories published in 2001.

True Crime Byline: A Politician Murdered his Ex Wife. We Should Have Seen it Coming   Looking back on the murder of JoAnn Wilson today, what's most surprising is that we were surprised it happened at all.

True Crime Byline podcast: The Article that Blew Up a Murder Trial   Listen to this podcast episode for the shocking story of how a National Post article about a teen vampire led to a mistrial, as told by reporter Joseph Brean

Douglas Todd: Canadian cops far less likely to kill, or be killed, than U.S. counterparts   Analysis: Canadian police are often accused of using excessive force, but a U.S. officer is five times more likely than a Canadian cop to shoot and kill.

Review: Bell Grandstand Show rivals the nightly fireworks for sound and colour   The 2022 Bell Grandstand Show is an explosion of lights, colour, sounds and movement, fittingly capped off with a spectacular fireworks display. In his song Bring Down the House, Brody sings about being that simple country boy who drives a truck but also has money in his pocket, a beautiful girl on his arm and the ability to bring down the house, which he certainly does. The Grandstand stage was made for his powerhouse talents. Amplified’s second headliner, award-winning trick roper Loop Rawlins, arrived on the back of a silver pickup truck and gave his ropes a good workout. He was joined by the Young Canadians who took up lassos and joined him for a most impressive finale to their number. The show’s final headliner, Canadian Indigenous singer Shawnee Kish gave Amplified a true adrenaline fix with her hit song I Got It Bad and then really amped things up with a meteoric rendition of Saturday Night. Kish returned for Amplified’s big finale where she sang and moved through fountains of water and mini towers of flames, eventually joined by the entire cast minus Dean Brody.

Brooks: Deerfoot City hosts PARK’s Block Party   PARK, which stands for Promoting Artists/Redefining Kulture (PARK), creates opportunities for developing fashion designers, artists, small businesses and creatives across Canada by raising awareness. Readers may recall innovative PARK events over the years at venues as varied as Studio Bell, Spruce Meadows, Chinook Centre Parkade, Brookfield Place and the George C. King Bridge in East Village, to name a few.

Steve Earle blazes his own outlaw trail — with guidance from mentors   Earle has never fit into the conservative confines of Nashville. But he has never given the impression that he cares all that much, either
Calgary-raised actor Praneet Akilla flying high as heroic pilot in TV drama SkyMed   Not long after graduating from McGill University, Praneet Akilla lost his iron ring.

Three-time Olympic gold medallist Rebecca Johnston to tutor future Flames as guest coach   This could be a nerve-wracking week for some of the Calgary Flames’ development-campers. They’re all chasing a dream, and this is an opportunity to showcase their skills for the squad they hope to someday represent.

RODEO NOTES: Clayton Sellars dominating in Stampede bull riding   Clayton Sellars is off to Showdown Sunday.

Style Therapy: From rebels to rockers, denim suits us all   It’s safe to say we all love jeans. They have influenced culture and style in the last two centuries probably more than we realize. Jeans have been worn by miners, cowboys, Hollywood stars, rebels, rockers and influencers. Almost all fashion designers and luxury labels have featured jeans in their collections.
Entertain in style in the great outdoors   Smokers, gas barbecues, big screen televisions help to set the scene.
Generous glazing floods the Cambridge floor plan with natural light   Trico Homes' show home in Midtown, Airdrie, showcases a comfortable and spacious family home.
White: Will Kelowna be Western Canada's next big and tall city?   Kelowna is growing up with high rise projects, and the city is attracting more millennials than any other age group.
June homes sales lose momentum to higher borrowing costs   "We expect to see more monthly slippage ... because gains were so strong at the start of the year,” says Ann-Marie Lurie of CREB.
Roe v. HOV: Pregnant Texas woman claims fetus counts as passenger   If a fetus is conferred legal protections as a human being, then that human is also a passenger, right?
BMW charges monthly fee for adaptive headlights in South Korea   Billing for items like adaptive cruise control doesn’t make any cents, but it's likely to make Bimmer plenty of dollars

Hyundai recalls Ioniq to fix part replaced in prior recall   There may be a loose connection in the power relay assembly under the rear seat, which could pose a fire risk
Does mindfulness meditation really reduce pain?   Participants taught to meditate before experiencing painful stimuli reported a 33% drop in pain unpleasantness and 32% reduction in intensity.
What are the signs of Magnesium deficiency?   More than one-third of Canadian adults aren't getting enough magnesium.

Parkour: Is this workout worth it?   Parkour combines cardiovascular endurance, strength training, balance and a quest for creativity that isn't seen in many other workouts — as long as you avoid falling from heights and slamming into the concrete.

Healthy Eating: Almonds, the favourite super food with a poisonous cousin   Eating sweet almonds is linked to a lower risk of developing obesity, coronary heart disease and cardiovascular disease. Bitter almonds, however, contain glycoside amygdalin — which is poisonous to humans.

'ALS will be a treatable chronic disease': New treatment slows disease progression and increases life expectancy   McGill University's Dr. Angela Genge is optimistic about what the future holds for amyotrophic lateral sclerosis care: "Every step gives our patients more time, better function for longer, better care every year."
